column4: Inaccurate Site Area selected, resulting in no traffic entering the test.

column4: Client request to remove calendar icon from CTA due to the action no longer being scheduling.
column9: No design provided. Calendar icon in CTA historically suggests that it is a "Scheduler" CTA. The action for this CTA changed to Learn More leading to an information page so the calendar icon is no longer needed.

column4: National Homepage showing two pause buttons, Local Pages missing See All Testimonials link.
column9: The experiment was implemented on the National Homepage without being communicated to the team. However, this experiment applies to Local Homepages as well and it was not found to be implemented on those pages.
column14: Send to development to have adjustments made. After reviewing and adjusting the site area from National + Local to only Local (where the experiment is not implemented) it functioned as expected.

column4: Existing deployment broke - content displaying on the page as [Object object] instead of expected carousels of content.
column9: An update from Convert's Legacy tracking script caused a major descrepancy. The usage of $ (convert's jQuery helper) changed how it interacts with elements in the DOM- changing the element from an object to a string.
column14: Adjust the code to use native methods instead of relying on Convert's $ helper.

column14: Send back to development with clear instructions on which images to use.

column4: Follow up issue related to the deployment fix logged on 12/4. Fix had been completed and shared with client but not approved for relaunch. Deployment remained paused while it was believed by PM and potentially the client to be live and malfunctioning.
column9: Deployment paused due to missing client approval, but assumed to be live. Section was not redesigned as expected because deployment was inactive- not due to code issues.
column14: Deployment reactivated after PM approval. Once active, section displayed and behaved as expected without additional changes.

column4: Converts updates to depracating jQuery have spanned issues across multiple deployments. This one particularly was impacted by the short form not submitting properly and not displaying the expected behavior of submit and continue.
column9: MRE CF 07.2 developed issues regarding form leads submitting and submit and continue after Convert depracated jQuery in their recent update.
column14: MRE CF 07.2 was triaged on 12/18/25 with issues appearing to be resolved by adding a limited number of checks onto site areas.

column4: Calendar step not allowing users to schedule appointments in 2026.
column9: The code was hardcoding the year 2025 instead of gathering the new year from the API. By replacing the year with 2025, it resulted in no availability for scheduling causing all the dates in January/February 2026 to appear as unavailable.
column14: Send back to development to have the code adjusted to accept the dynamic date instead of hardcoding it. Additional updates to replace all instances of jQuery were completed as well.

column4: Short Forms allow users to proceed without entering a last name. This prevents Submit & Continue from behaving as expected as it will get stuck on Step 1 instead of passing over it.
column9: Client reported that users only entering a first name will not be skipped over step 1 in the lead flow. The expectation is that all users will enter a first and last name so that the step skipping experience is universal when coming from any short form on the page.
column14: Send to development to have validation added to the form that will require users to enter a first and last name before proceeding.
